title: `skopeo copy` fails with "requested access to resource is denied"
body:
I am trying to copy an image built locally using docker (`docker build -t tagless:test .`) to a directory using skopeo but keep getting an access denied error:

```
[rose@fedora home]$ skopeo copy docker://tagless:test dir:/home/rose/.tern/temp
FATA[0001] initializing source docker://tagless:test: reading manifest test in docker.io/library/tagless: requested access to the resource is denied
```

I am logged in to docker. How do I authenticate the copy?

Thanks in advance.

comments.body:
Thanks for your report.

Run `skopeo --debug copy …`, see where it is reading credentials from, if anywhere.

Thanks for your quick response @mtrmac. Here's the debug output:

```
ternenv) [rose@fedora tern]$ skopeo --debug copy docker://tagless:test dir:/home/rose/.tern/temp
DEBU[0000] overwriting existing container image directory "/home/rose/.tern/temp" 
DEBU[0000] Using registries.d directory /etc/containers/registries.d 
DEBU[0000] Loading registries configuration "/etc/containers/registries.conf" 
DEBU[0000] Loading registries configuration "/etc/containers/registries.conf.d/000-shortnames.conf" 
DEBU[0000] Trying to access "docker.io/library/tagless:test" 
DEBU[0000] No credentials matching docker.io/library/tagless found in /run/user/1000/containers/auth.json 
DEBU[0000] No credentials matching docker.io/library/tagless found in /home/rose/.config/containers/auth.json 
DEBU[0000] Found credentials for docker.io/library/tagless in credential helper containers-auth.json in file /home/rose/.docker/config.json 
DEBU[0000]  Lookaside configuration: using "default-docker" configuration 
DEBU[0000]  No signature storage configuration found for docker.io/library/tagless:test, using built-in default file:///home/rose/.local/share/containers/sigstore 
DEBU[0000] Looking for TLS certificates and private keys in /etc/docker/certs.d/docker.io 
DEBU[0000]  Sigstore attachments: using "default-docker" configuration 
DEBU[0000] GET https://registry-1.docker.io/v2/         
DEBU[0000] Ping https://registry-1.docker.io/v2/ status 401 
DEBU[0000] GET https://auth.docker.io/token?account=rosejudge&scope=repository%3Alibrary%2Ftagless%3Apull&service=registry.docker.io 
DEBU[0000] GET https://registry-1.docker.io/v2/library/tagless/manifests/test 
DEBU[0001] Detected insufficient_scope error, will retry request with updated scope 
DEBU[0001] GET https://auth.docker.io/token?account=rosejudge&scope=repository%3Alibrary%2Ftagless%3Apull&scope=repository%3Alibrary%2Ftagless%3Apull&service=registry.docker.io 
DEBU[0001] GET https://registry-1.docker.io/v2/library/tagless/manifests/test 
DEBU[0001] Content-Type from manifest GET is "application/json" 
DEBU[0001] Discarding non-primary errors:               
DEBU[0001]   unauthorized: authentication required      
DEBU[0001] Accessing "docker.io/library/tagless:test" failed: reading manifest test in docker.io/library/tagless: requested access to the resource is denied 
FATA[0001] initializing source docker://tagless:test: reading manifest test in docker.io/library/tagless: requested access to the resource is denied 

```

Oh… a _locally-built image_.

That’s not `docker://tagless:test` (an image uploaded to `docker.io/library/tagless:test`). Try using `docker-daemon:tagless:test`.
A-ha! That's what I was missing. Sorry about that. Thanks for your time and the solution :)
